RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-12-526396, CGC-13-531045 WITH CASE CGC-12-526395 is denied without prejudice to being heard by the trial judge. All three cases will be given the same trial date. The trial date in case 526395 will be continued to April 18, 2016, the same date as the trial date in cases 526396 and 531045. The request to stay the two earlier filed cases pending the conclusion of 531045 was not properly made by way of a motion or cross-motion and has not been considered by the court. The motion to consolidate is denied on the grounds that: 1) moving parties did not show any change in circumstances since the denial of the earlier motion to consolidate and 2) moving parties failed to provide any evidence to support their assertions that essentially the same products are involved in all three cases. There being no objection by any party, the Court shall recommend to the Presiding Judge and Complex Litigation judges that these three cases be singly-assigned for case management purposes. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT MCCORMICK & COMPANY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/HK)

SARAH GOEKLER, TEL. NO. (530) 941-2621, COURT REPORTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH's Motion To Compel Defendant Safeway Inc.S Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions is Granted. All General Objections are stricken. (See CCP sec. 2031.210-240.) As to the Special Interrogatories, all objections are overruled. Defendant shall serve a verified response to Interrogatories #1-56 no later than Sept. 15, 2014. As to the Request for Production of Documents, all objections are overruled. Defendant shall serve a verified response and produce documents to Requests #1-24 and 26 no later than September 15, 2014. Any document withheld on the claim of privilege must be listed in a privilege log pursuant to CCP sec. 2031.240. Defendant need not list any attorney-client privilege document that was created after Safeway was served with the lawsuit. As to the Requests for Admission, all objections are overruled. Defendant shall serve a verified response to Requests #1-25 no later than September 15, 2014. As to the Form Interrogatories, all objections are overruled. Defendant shall serve a verified response to Form Interrogatories # 1.1, 3.7, 15.1, and 17.1 no later than September 15, 2014. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted. The court finds that defendant Safeway failed to meet its obligations under CCP 2030.210-240, CCP 2031.210-240 and CCP 2033.210-240 in responding to discovery. See Clement v. Alegre (2009) 177 CA4th 1277. The court further finds that defendant Safeway failed to meet and confer in good faith prior to the July 29, 2014 hearing. See Townsend v. Superior Court (1998) 61 CA 4th 1431. Sanctions in the amount of $3560 for attorneys' fees and the cost for filing the motion to be made payable to Center for Environmental Health and their attorneys of record, Lexington Law Group to be paid within 30 days of the hearing. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sarah Goekler, CSR # 13446 =302/eg

RULING: The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification. PLAINTIFF CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-12-526396, CGC-13-531045 WITH CASE CGC-12-526395 is denied without prejudice. The Court cannot order "pretrial consolidation." In Sanchez v. Sup. Crt., (1988) 203 Cal.App.3d 1391, 1396, the court explains that there can only be complete consolidation or consolidation for trial purposes.
